title: Expo Framework Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Expo Go, EAS Builds, and OTA Updates.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of utilizing 'Expo' over the bare 'React Native CLI' when starting a new mobile project?

options.text: Expo forces the app to render using an HTML WebView.
Expo abstracts away all the agonizing complexity of bridging native iOS (Xcode) and Android (Studio) code, allowing developers to build complex native apps writing 100% pure JavaScript/TypeScript.
Expo allows you to write PHP to build mobile apps.
There are no advantages; Expo is entirely deprecated.

questions.question: During development, how does the 'Expo Go' mobile application drastically speed up developer productivity?

options.text: It writes the React Native code for you using AI.
It allows developers to simply scan a QR code to instantly stream their live development app via Wi-Fi to a physical phone with hot-reloading, avoiding tedious physical USB cable compiling.
It bypasses the Apple App Store temporarily.
It automatically generates automated unit tests.

questions.question: You are a developer using a Microsoft Windows computer. You need to compile your new React Native app into an `.ipa` file to upload to the Apple App Store. How does Expo solve your hardware problem?

options.text: By rendering a virtual Mac operating system using Docker locally.
By utilizing 'EAS Build' (Expo Application Services), which packages your code and compiles it remotely on Expo's cloud-hosted Mac servers, returning the finished Apple file to your PC.
Windows PCs actually can compile iOS code natively.
By converting the app into an Android APK.

questions.question: What is an 'Over-The-Air' (OTA) Update in the context of Expo?

options.text: A feature that allows you to push Javascript fixes (like typos) directly to users' phones instantly over the internet, completely bypassing the notoriously slow 3-day Apple App Store review process.
A method to update the physical iOS operating system.
A system for updating API backend servers without downtime.
A method requiring users to plug their phone into a PC.

questions.question: If your application requires accessing the phone's native GPS Location hardware, how do you handle this securely using Expo?

options.text: You must manually edit the `AndroidManifest.xml` and `Info.plist` files via Java.
You cannot; Expo does not support native APIs.
By running `npx expo install expo-location`, which automatically installs the pre-built, robust native bridging packages instantly without touching native files.
By utilizing a web browser geolocation hook.
